subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF EFF/ECF/RSF reviews, World Bank, V-Dem, Freedom House, RSF, TI) + international press (Reuters, AFP, France 24, Washington Post, Al Jazeera) and UN Security Council/ICG on Sahel spillover.

Macro star, political backslider: ~7.5% growth and an on-track IMF program against a decade of Talon-era opposition exclusion, jailed opponents, and an April-2026 succession election (Wadagni ~94%) that observers called neither free nor fair; the WAEMU CFA franc is pegged to the euro.

Standout features: standout WAEMU macro performance and the GDIZ industrialization push; a hollowed-out competitive electoral process; and a sharp 2025 JNIM jihadist surge in the north (April attack the deadliest in a decade).

economic.notes: Standout WAEMU macro performance - ~7.5% growth, deficit at the 3% norm, low peg-anchored inflation - with cotton/oil-transit concentration the main risk.

fiscal_state.notes: Frontloaded consolidation cut the deficit to ~3.1% of GDP in 2024, meeting the WAEMU 3% norm a year early; debt revised up to ~60.5% of GDP but moderate-risk under an on-track EFF/ECF/RSF program. (IMF Feb-2026)

monetary_independence.notes: The CFA-franc euro peg delivers credibility but zero autonomy under a France-guaranteed, BCEAO-pooled regime. (BCEAO/IMF)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The CFA franc is not a reserve currency. (definitional)

inflation.notes: Low and peg-anchored (~1-3%) - a structural strength. (IMF)

banking_stability.notes: Sound within the WAEMU framework, with sovereign-exposure concentration the main watch-item. (IMF)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Africa's top cotton producer moving up the value chain via the GDIZ zone (raw-cotton export ban from May 2025) and a Niger-oil transit pipeline; offset by commodity concentration and the closed Niger border. (IMF; France 24; African Energy)

institutional.notes: Strong macro/statistical capacity and civil service, undercut by an instrumentalized judiciary and a sharply pressured press.

rule_of_law.notes: Courts used against opponents - the CRIET special court jailed presidential hopefuls and, in Jan-2025, sentenced Talon ally Boko and ex-minister Homeky to 20 years over an alleged coup plot. (HRW; Reuters; AFP)

statistical_integrity.notes: Strong and IMF-engaged through frequent program reviews. (IMF)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Comparatively capable and reform-driven, anchoring the GDIZ and fiscal-management gains. (IMF/World Bank)

press_freedom.notes: Sharply eroded over the Talon decade - digital-code prosecutions of journalists and a poor RSF standing. (RSF)

political_social.notes: Competitive politics hollowed out by opposition exclusion, atop a young population and a stressed northern periphery.

political_stability.notes: An April-2026 succession election handed Talon's finance minister Wadagni ~94% with no credible opposition on the ballot - observers called it neither free nor fair; orderly but legitimacy-thin. (Washington Post; France 24)

civil_liberties.notes: Eroded - opposition figures jailed or exiled, candidacy rules (15% MP/mayor endorsements) used to lock out rivals, and curbs on assembly and expression. (Freedom House; V-Dem)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: No systematic abuse of foreigners; regional migration and Niger-border refugee/displacement pressures are the main strain. (UNHCR; observed)

social_cohesion.notes: Generally cohesive, but a north-south and Fulani-grievance fault line that jihadists are exploiting. (ICG)

demographics.notes: Very young and fast-growing - a dividend with a heavy jobs strain. (UN)

geopolitical.notes: A Western-aligned ECOWAS member but on the front line of southward Sahel jihadist spillover and a Niger-border standoff.

alliance_reliability.notes: A stable Western/EU- and US-aligned ECOWAS member, though the Niger junta dispute strained a key neighbor. (observed)

conflict_involvement.notes: A severe 2025 JNIM surge in the north - ~35 soldiers killed in Jan, ~54 in April (the deadliest attack in a decade), drawing a UN Security Council statement. (UN; ICG; Critical Threats)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: An open WAEMU/CFA regime, unsanctioned; the closed Niger border is a bilateral friction. (IMF; observed)

physical_practical.notes: Improving logistics around Cotonou and the GDIZ, against flood/coastal climate exposure, a weak health system, and northern insecurity.

climate.notes: Coastal erosion, seasonal flooding, and northern drought/desertification; an IMF Resilience and Sustainability Facility addresses it. (IMF RSF; World Bank)

healthcare.notes: Weak and underfunded, with low coverage and reliance on external support. (World Bank/WHO)

infrastructure.notes: Improving - the Cotonou port, GDIZ, and the Niger transit pipeline - though northern roads and power lag. (AfDB; World Bank)

crime_safety.notes: Moderate in the south; the north carries jihadist/kidnapping and travel-advisory risk. (OSAC; observed)

summary: Benin is a WAEMU macro star paired with a political backslider. Talon's decade delivered ~7.5% growth (its highest yet), a deficit cut to the 3% regional norm a year early, low euro-peg-anchored inflation, an on-track IMF EFF/ECF/RSF program, and a genuine industrialization push via the Glo-Djigbe (GDIZ) zone and a Niger-oil transit pipeline. But competitive politics has been hollowed out: opposition figures jailed (the CRIET court) or exiled, candidacy rules weaponized, and an April-2026 succession election that handed finance minister Wadagni ~94% with no credible opponent and was judged neither free nor fair. A sharp 2025 JNIM jihadist surge in the north - the April attack the deadliest in a decade - plus a weak health system and flood exposure round out a negatively skewed profile where strong assets/currency fundamentals sit beside poor governance and security tails.
